audit_title: OpenCode Desktop App — Feature Parity & UX Audit

feature_parity_gaps.description: TUI CLI features/commands that are missing, partially present, or equivalently implemented in the desktop app UI

gaps.notes: Plugin system supports npm packages with hooks. Desktop has no plugin browser, installer, or config UI. Users must use CLI. IPC channel get-desktop-plugin-config exists in preload but no UI.
Full GitHub App OAuth flow, workflow file generation, CI/CD event handler mode (issue_comment, PR review). 1649-line module. Desktop has no GitHub integration UI at all.
Create PRs, push branches from local changes. Desktop has review panel but no PR creation/publishing flow.
Desktop has dialog-select-mcp for using MCP tools in session, but no full management UI for adding/removing/configuring MCP servers. TUI supports 6 subcommands.
Export sanitizes sessions (redacts API keys), import parses share URLs and JSON. Desktop has fork/share dialogs but no export/import feature boundary.
Displays session counts, tool usage rankings, model usage breakdowns. 393-line module. Desktop has session viz panel but no aggregate statistics.
TUI supports creating custom agents with system prompts, model selection, provider config. Desktop has agent selection dropdown but no agent creation/config UI.
Desktop has dialog-manage-models and model selection, but no provider-specific model listing, verbose mode, or model capability inspection.
Desktop has DialogConnectProvider for some providers but TUI supports full OAuth flow with plugin auth hooks, custom provider config, and org-scoped provider management.
Desktop has account-related UI but lacks org switching, full login/logout flow management, and console URL configuration.
Open SQLite/Postgres console, run queries, view migration status. Primarily developer-facing; acceptable gap for desktop.
711-line migration system: SQLite→Postgres/PGlite migration with verification, rollback, FK auditing. One-shot migration tool, not interactive; gap is acceptable.
Desktop has auto-updater (electron-updater) but no manual check/apply UI in settings. TUI upgrade command provides interactive upgrade flow.
Desktop app can be uninstalled via OS mechanisms. TUI uninstall is more thorough (cleans shell config, data dirs). Equivalent for practical purposes.

gaps.tui_feature: Server mode controls (opencode serve / web / acp)
gaps.notes: Desktop runs server automatically via sidecar. No UI for controlling server port, network binding, or ACP protocol mode.
Generates TypeScript SDK from OpenAPI specs. Developer tool; acceptable gap.
TUI automatically compacts context window and detects overflow. Desktop session UI has no indication of context pressure or compaction events.
TUI supports @general to delegate complex multi-step tasks to a subagent. Desktop composer supports @mentions but only for files/agents, not the general subagent.
TUI has a skill tool that loads specialized instructions. Desktop has no skill browser or loading UI.
TUI has experimental native (local) LLM runtime. Desktop depends on remote providers or server-managed models.
Desktop has permission provider and dialog for tool permissions, but TUI has finer-grained ALWAYS/ASK/NEVER modes. UI for customizing per-tool permissions per-session is limited.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: Single dialog (dialog-onboarding) shown on first launch. Basic provider setup and project selection.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Multi-step guided onboarding: (1) Welcome + product tour, (2) Provider/API key setup with inline validation, (3) Project selection with recent projects, (4) First prompt with template suggestions. Include progress indicator and skip option.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: Command palette exists but scope is limited to basic navigation and settings.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Extensible command palette with fuzzy search, recent commands, keyboard-only navigation, plugin-contributed commands, and mode indicator (commands vs. files vs. symbols).

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: No search across sessions. Session list is basic with name/date display.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Full-text search across all sessions with keyword highlighting. Filter by date range, agent used, model, tool usage. Pin/bookmark important sessions. Bulk session management (archive, delete, export).

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: No search within a session timeline.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: In-session search with keyboard shortcut (Cmd+F). Highlight matching messages. Filter by message type (tool calls, code changes, user prompts, AI responses). Jump-to-match navigation.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: Review panel shows git/branch/turn diffs but no side-by-side session comparison.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Select two sessions and view a diff of: messages, files changed, tools used, model responses. Useful for before/after comparisons and regression analysis.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: Review panel shows diffs as read-only. Users accept/reject via buttons.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Inline code editing within diffs: modify the proposed changes before accepting. Stage individual hunks instead of all-or-nothing. Keyboard shortcuts for accept/reject/edit.
references: GitHub PR file view with edit

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: Session viz panel shows activity feed but no visual file change timeline.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Timeline view showing which files were modified at each turn. Color-coded by operation (create/modify/delete). Click to jump to the specific diff. Filter by file type or directory.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: Composer supports @mentions (files/agents), /commands, image paste/drop, shell mode toggle, followup queue.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Add: inline code block insertion with syntax highlighting, typed command suggestions with descriptions, multi-file drag-and-drop with preview, voice input (Web Speech API), snippet/template picker, and auto-complete for agent instructions.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: Multi-tab terminal with drag-and-drop, WebSocket transport, xterm-like emulation.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Add: true xterm.js integration with full CSI support, 256-color themes, font ligatures, search (Ctrl+Shift+F), split panes, persistent sessions across app restarts, and environment variable inspection.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: Dialog-select-mcp shows configured MCP servers but no discovery mechanism.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: In-app MCP server browser: discover public MCP servers, one-click add, authentication status badges, server health indicators, and tool preview. Community- contributed indexes.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: No plugin management UI at all.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Browse, install, and configure plugins from npm. Show plugin capabilities, permissions, and version info. One-click install from search results. Plugin settings panel.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: 35 dynamic themes using Oklch color space via dropdown selector. No customization.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Visual theme editor: tweak colors, preview in real-time, save custom themes. Import/export theme JSON. Community theme gallery. Adaptive theme based on system appearance.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: Settings-keybinds panel lists shortcuts but no in-app hints or shortcut discovery mode.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Press-and-hold modifier key to show shortcut overlay. Context-sensitive tooltips showing shortcuts. Shortcut conflict detection. Custom keybinding editor with search.

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: Basic progress indicators during LLM calls. Session shows activity feed.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Detailed progress: current tool being executed, estimated time remaining, editable tool queue (reorder/cancel pending tools), streaming token display with statistics (tokens/sec, cost estimate).

ux_improvement_opportunities.current_state: System notifications on completion, sound effects, basic notification provider.
ux_improvement_opportunities.opportunity: Notification center: history of all desktop notifications, action buttons (open session, retry, view diff), focus mode (mute during deep work), custom notification rules per event type.

design_system_concerns.concern: Two parallel design systems (OC-2 v1 and V2) with different token naming conventions exist simultaneously
design_system_concerns.recommendation: Establish a single source of truth for design tokens. Consolidate v1 components into v2 or vice versa. Create a token dictionary mapping old→new names. Add runtime deprecation warnings for v1 token usage.

design_system_concerns.concern: No formal design token documentation file or schema
design_system_concerns.recommendation: Create a canonical design token JSON schema in docs/schemas/. Document all tokens with their values, usage guidelines, and which components consume them. Generate TypeScript types from the schema.

Integrate axe-core or similar a11y testing into the testing pipeline. Add baseline a11y assertions to all existing story files. Use @kobalte/core's built-in a11y guarantees but verify at the render level.
Adopt Chromatic, Percy, or Playwright screenshot tests for all 81 story files. Establish a visual baseline and diff workflow in CI. Tag stories with viewport variants.

design_system_concerns.concern: 2 of 55 v1 components lack story files; 2 of 26 v2 components lack story files
design_system_concerns.recommendation: Audit which components are missing stories. Add stories for the missing 4 components. Enforce 100% story coverage in CI with a lint rule or test.

design_system_concerns.concern: 105 custom SVG icons (v1) plus separate v2 icon sprite — overlapping icon sets with no deduplication
design_system_concerns.recommendation: Merge v1 and v2 icon sets into a single canonical icon library. Remove duplicates, standardize on sizing (16/20/24dp), and generate a single optimized sprite sheet. Add TypeScript icon name types.

design_system_concerns.concern: Tailwind CSS v4 config and custom theme tokens are not versioned or published
design_system_concerns.recommendation: Extract theme configuration into a versioned npm package (@opencode-ai/design-tokens). Publish alongside the UI library. Consumers should import from a single source.

design_system_concerns.concern: @kobalte/core version may lag behind upstream, creating a11y regression risk
design_system_concerns.recommendation: Pin @kobalte/core to a specific release and automate dependency upgrade testing with a11y assertions. Monitor upstream for breaking changes and security fixes.

architectural_observations.observation: IPC channel installCli exposed in preload bridge but no ipcMain.handle registered — renders calls from renderer will hang indefinitely
architectural_observations.implication: Any renderer code that calls window.electronAPI.installCli() will hang as there's no main-process handler to respond
architectural_observations.recommendation: Either implement the installCli handler in ipc.ts or remove the channel from preload. Audit all IPC channels for orphaned handlers.

architectural_observations.observation: Desktop renderer (SolidJS) and TUI CLI (Effect-TS) use entirely different UI frameworks and paradigms
architectural_observations.implication: No shared UI code between desktop and TUI. Feature development must be done twice for CLI and desktop parity. Bug fixes and UX improvements don't transfer between surfaces.
architectural_observations.recommendation: Accept this as architectural intent (different surfaces serve different users). Establish a clear feature parity matrix and maintain it as a living document. Prioritize desktop for rich GUI interactions and TUI for scripting/CI.

architectural_observations.observation: Desktop app depends on sidecar HTTP server for all LLM and business logic operations
architectural_observations.implication: Desktop cannot function without the sidecar process. If the sidecar crashes or hangs, the UI becomes non-functional (ConnectionError screen). Single point of failure.
architectural_observations.recommendation: Add sidecar health monitoring with auto-restart. Implement stale detection (no heartbeat within N seconds → show recovery UI rather than frozen state). Consider in-process fallback for read-only operations.

architectural_observations.observation: Desktop menu system uses both Electron native menus (macOS) and a React/Solid custom menu (Windows/Linux titlebar overlay)
architectural_observations.implication: Menu actions are centralized in desktop-menu-actions.ts but the rendering path differs per platform, creating potential for platform-specific bugs.
architectural_observations.recommendation: Unify menu action dispatch regardless of rendering path. Add platform-specific integration tests. Document the menu action flow: user → native/custom menu → IPC → desktop-menu-actions → sidecar.

architectural_observations.observation: Postgres migration (migrate-to-pg) is in progress but desktop app has no awareness of the dual storage backend
architectural_observations.implication: Desktop may read stale SQLite data while migration runs, or may attempt operations that assume SQLite schema. No UI for migration progress or rollback.
architectural_observations.recommendation: Surface migration state to desktop via IPC. Add migration progress indicator to settings. Block write operations during migration. Eventually deprecate SQLite path once PGlite/Postgres is stable.

architectural_observations.observation: Desktop app has 13+ Context providers in a nested provider tree (provider-hell pattern)
architectural_observations.implication: Deeply nested context providers make debugging state issues harder. Adding new providers requires editing multiple wrapper components. Performance impact from context re-renders.
architectural_observations.recommendation: Consider consolidating related contexts (server-related, settings-related, session-related). Evaluate SolidJS context optimization patterns. Profile re-render impact of context changes.

architectural_observations.observation: Desktop app uses Electron 41.2 with electron-vite 5 — modern but fast-moving target
architectural_observations.implication: Electron 41 is new (2026). APromise-based IPC changes, utilityProcess API may still evolve. Auto-update targeting GitHub Releases is working but fragile across major Electron versions.
architectural_observations.recommendation: Pin Electron minor version. Maintain an upgrade checklist for each major Electron bump. Test full IPC flow after upgrade. Consider Electron security model updates (contextIsolation, sandbox).

architectural_observations.observation: UI library (55+26=81 components) has no formal versioning or changelog
architectural_observations.implication: Consumers (@opencode-ai/app, potential external consumers) cannot track breaking changes. Storybook stories serve as de facto docs but there's no migration guide between versions.
architectural_observations.recommendation: Add conventional commits and auto-changelog generation to @opencode-ai/ui. Publish a CHANGELOG.md. Add semantic versioning with major/minor/patch based on component API changes.

architectural_observations.observation: Desktop app uses @opencode-ai/sdk/v2 for API communication but the SDK itself is generated from OpenAPI specs
architectural_observations.implication: SDK regeneration may introduce breaking changes that are not caught until runtime. No TypeScript compile-time safety between SDK types and desktop usage patterns.
architectural_observations.recommendation: Add SDK integration tests that verify critical API calls work end-to-end. Consider code generation of type guards. Pin SDK version in desktop package.json and upgrade deliberately.

blockers_found.description: installCli IPC channel is registered in preload bridge (window.electronAPI.installCli) but no ipcMain.handle('installCli') handler exists in main process — any renderer code calling this will hang indefinitely
Two parallel design systems (v1 and v2) with different token naming cause component inconsistency — 81 components split across two systems with no clear migration path to v2
Desktop auto-updater logs spinning (stdout → stderr) from @electron/remote workaround pollutes stderr; error logged every poll cycle

items: Fix orphaned installCli IPC channel — add handler or remove from preload
Add in-session message search (Cmd+F) with highlight and jump-to-match navigation
Add export/import UI for sessions (JSON file picker, share URL import)
Add session statistics summary to session viz panel (message count, tool calls, tokens used)
Add context compaction indicator to message timeline header (warn when approaching context limit)
Add missing story files for the 4 components without stories
Add model provider-specific model listing to settings-models dialog
Surface migration state via IPC if Postgres migration is in progress
Plugin management UI — browse installed plugins, install new ones, configure plugin settings
Agent configuration UI — create/edit custom agents with system prompt, model, provider selection
MCP server management UI — full CRUD for MCP servers, auth status display, connect/disconnect flow
GitHub integration UI — connect GitHub App, view CI status, manage workflow files
PR management UI — create PR from session changes, push branch with commit, select base branch
Command palette expansion — plugin-contributed commands, fuzzy search, keyboard-only mode
Session search and filter — full-text search across sessions, filter by date/agent/model
Subagent delegation UI — invoke @general or custom subagents from the composer
Inline diff editing — modify proposed changes before accepting, stage individual hunks
Session diff/comparison — side-by-side session comparison for before/after analysis
Guided multi-step onboarding — welcome tour, provider setup, project selection with template prompts
MCP server browser — discover public MCP servers, one-click add, health indicators, tool preview
Plugin marketplace UI — browse, search, and install plugins from npm in-app
Design system consolidation — merge v1/v2 components into single system, formal design token schema
Automated a11y testing — axe-core integration in CI, 100% story coverage with a11y assertions
Visual regression testing — Playwright/Percy screenshots for all components
Theme customizer — visual editor, custom theme save/export, community theme gallery
Keyboard shortcut discoverability — hold-modifier overlay, shortcut conflict detection, custom keybind editor

deferred: Performance profiling of the virtualized message timeline (virtua/solid)
Security audit of IPC channels (all 42 channels) for privilege escalation
Auto-update reliability testing across platforms (macOS notarization, Windows code signing, Linux AppImage)
Desktop app memory usage analysis (Electron renderer memory, sidecar memory)
Cross-platform testing matrix (macOS ARM/x64, Windows, Linux deb/rpm/AppImage)
Telemetry and crash reporting data quality review
Sidecar startup time optimization and cold-start experience
Internationalization completeness audit (18 languages — verify all strings translated)
Detailed component-by-component design system migration plan from v1→v2
Electron 41 new API evaluation (Service Workers, Window Management, Keyboard Lock)
Deep link handler security review (opencode:// protocol injection risks)
Desktop app build pipeline review (electron-vite, packaging, CI artifact generation)
